name: Shapiro
classification_notes: Nearly monolinear strokes, grotesque roots similar to Helvetica. Available in standard and wide widths.
summary: A versatile neo-grotesque with nearly monolinear strokes, available in standard and wide widths across seven weights.
description: Shapiro draws from the grotesque tradition with clean, nearly monolinear letterforms suited to branding, packaging, advertising and editorial work. The superfamily spans 28 styles across two widths, with variable font options for maximum flexibility. Language support covers 26 languages across Latin script.
foundry_blurb: Shapiro features nearly monolinear strokes giving the family a sharp, modern look. With grotesque roots similar to Helvetica, it maintains versatility for diverse applications.

name: Sequel Sans
classification_notes: Swiss-style neo-grotesque inspired by Max Bill's work. Created in collaboration with the Max Bill Georges Vantongerloo Foundation. Five widths: standard, compressed, condensed, wide, extended.
summary: A comprehensive Swiss-style neo-grotesque in five widths, conceived in collaboration with the Max Bill Georges Vantongerloo Foundation.
description: Sequel Sans takes its inspiration from the typefaces associated with Max Bill, delivering a clean and adaptable family across 58 styles and five widths. The design emphasises direct, purposeful form and function in the Swiss modernist tradition, with variable font support for both text and display use. Language support covers 26 languages.
foundry_blurb: Conceived in collaboration with the Max Bill Georges Vantongerloo Foundation, Sequel Sans is a clean and adaptable Swiss family drawing inspiration from the typefaces used by Max Bill, emphasising direct and purposeful form and function for modern typography.

name: JT Collect
classification_notes: Blends geometric structure with traditional grotesque elements. Three optical sizes for text and display use.
summary: A hybrid sans-serif blending geometric structure with grotesque character, offered in three optical sizes across six weights.
description: JT Collect draws on German and Swiss typographic heritage to produce a contemporary industrial aesthetic. Three optical sizes adapt the design from text to display settings, while six weights give strong range for editorial, corporate and brand applications. Language coverage spans 26 languages.
foundry_blurb: A hybrid sans serif for the 21st century that blends geometric structure with traditional grotesque elements, drawing inspiration from German and Swiss typographic heritage.

name: Shapiro Class
classification_notes: Condensed companion to Shapiro. Eight weights named after boxing weight classes. Nearly monolinear strokes.
summary: A condensed neo-grotesque companion to Shapiro, with eight weights named after boxing weight classes for a distinctive personality.
description: Shapiro Class takes the monolinear neo-grotesque character of Shapiro and applies it in a condensed format, suited to display headlines, packaging and space-constrained layouts. Its eight weights use boxing nomenclature, running from Fly through Super Heavy, with italic variants across the range. Two variable fonts are included with the superfamily.
foundry_blurb: Shapiro Class is a condensed neo-grotesque featuring nearly monolinear strokes with a contemporary aesthetic, versatile for diverse applications from branding to editorial design.

name: Shapiro Max
classification_notes: Ultra-heavy two-style extension of the Shapiro family. Black and Ultra weights only.
summary: A two-weight ultra-heavy extension of Shapiro, designed for bold display and impactful branding work.
description: Shapiro Max offers the Black and Ultra weights of the Shapiro neo-grotesque, providing maximum visual impact for display headlines, packaging and large-scale branding. Thick, nearly monolinear strokes carry the same modernist clarity as the core Shapiro family. Supports 26 languages.
foundry_blurb: Shapiro Max is a bold neo-grotesque with thick, nearly monolinear strokes delivering a contemporary aesthetic for branding, packaging, advertising and editorial applications.

name: Temper Wide
classification_notes: Unbracketed serifs, square dots, flat terminals. Monolinear strokes. Industrial character. Wide proportions.
summary: A wide neo-grotesque slab with unbracketed serifs and square dots, built for editorial and advertising use with an industrial edge.
description: Temper Wide emphasises modern neutrality through monolinear strokes, unembellished unbracketed serifs and flat terminals. The four-weight family suits editorial design, advertising campaigns and brands seeking a practical yet distinctive voice. Two variable fonts are included. Language coverage spans 26 languages.
foundry_blurb: Temper Wide is a modern neutral typeface featuring monolinear strokes, unbracketed serifs, square dots and flat terminals, suited for editorial design, advertising and brands seeking distinctive practicality.

name: Darbee Legend
classification_notes: Condensed neo-grotesque with boxy forms and angled terminals. Efficiently modern character.
summary: A condensed neo-grotesque with boxy forms and angled terminals, projecting an efficiently modern character across four weights.
description: Darbee Legend brings a distinctive boxy, industrial quality to the condensed grotesque form. Angled terminals differentiate it from more neutral contemporaries, making it effective for headlines, packaging and branding where compact yet legible letterforms are needed. Four weights with italics, plus two variable fonts.
foundry_blurb: Darbee Legend is a condensed neo-grotesque featuring boxy forms and angled terminals, described as efficiently modern and suitable for clear, direct typography.

name: Bill Corporate
classification_notes: Geometric sans with pointy apexes (not flat) and archetypal proportions. Inspired by Max Bill lettering. Distinct from Futura in details.
summary: A geometric sans with pointy apexes and archetypal proportions, rooted in Max Bill's modernist vocabulary and suited to corporate and wayfinding applications.
description: Bill Corporate channels Max Bill's lettering principles into a broad 27-style family spanning Hairline to Extra Bold with italics. Pointy apexes, rather than the flat cuts found in Futura, give it a timeless formal vocabulary distinct from its modernist peers. Strong for corporate identity, wayfinding, signage and digital interfaces. Supports 26 languages.
foundry_blurb: Bill Corporate is a geometric sans serif with pointy apexes and archetypal proportions, aimed at a timeless formal vocabulary rooted in modernist design principles, suitable for corporate branding, wayfinding, signage and digital interfaces.

name: Bill Display
classification_notes: Display companion to Bill Corporate. Minimalist letterforms, pointy apexes, archetypal proportions. Designed for striking visual impact at large sizes.
summary: A display-optimised geometric face inspired by Max Bill's lettering, with minimalist forms and pointy apexes across 17 weights.
description: Bill Display strips back to the essentials of modernist lettering for use at headline scale, with minimalist letterforms, pointy apexes and archetypal proportions that avoid current design trends. Seventeen weights give strong range from Hairline through Heavy, including five grades at the lightest end. Small capitals and stylistic alternates add typographic utility. Supports 26 languages.
foundry_blurb: Bill Display features minimalist letterforms, pointy apexes instead of flat ones, and archetypal proportions designed for striking visual impact without following current design trends.

name: JT Energy
classification_notes: Geometric sans inspired by Paul Renner and Arno Drescher. Includes two Placard-style weights. Text and display applications.
summary: A geometric sans for the 21st century inspired by Paul Renner and Arno Drescher, with two distinctive Placard weights in its nine-weight range.
description: JT Energy channels the spirit of classic mid-century geometric design into a contemporary nine-weight family, with two distinctive No. 77 and No. 78 Placard weights that evoke early European lettering. Refined details set it apart from similar geometric faces, making it suitable for brands, magazines, packaging and financial reporting. Supports 26 languages.
foundry_blurb: JT Energy is a geometric sans serif for the 21st century, inspired by the work of Paul Renner and Arno Drescher, combining striking clarity with an almost industrial quality and refined details that distinguish it from similar typefaces.

name: Sequel Geo
classification_notes: Geometric-grotesque hybrid bridging 1920s German and 1950s Swiss design. Circular punctuation, simplified t, single-storey a. Two widths.
summary: A geometric-grotesque hybrid bridging 1920s German and 1950s Swiss modernism, with circular punctuation and a single-storey a in two widths.
description: Sequel Geo sits at the intersection of geometric and grotesque traditions, sharing DNA with Sequel Sans while introducing its own character through circular punctuation, a simplified t and a single-storey a. Nine weights with matching italics span the full range across standard and extended widths. Language coverage includes 26 languages.
foundry_blurb: Sequel Geo is a geometric and grotesque hybrid inspired by modernist design, bridging 1920s Germany to 1950s Switzerland, with distinctive elements like circular punctuation, a simplified t and a one-story a.

name: Sequel Rounded
classification_notes: Rounded Swiss neo-grotesque. Two styles only: Wide Display and Wide Text. Created in collaboration with Max Bill Georges Vantongerloo Foundation.
summary: A rounded Swiss neo-grotesque in wide proportions, developed in collaboration with the Max Bill Georges Vantongerloo Foundation.
description: Sequel Rounded applies rounded terminals to the Swiss grotesque tradition, carrying the influence of Max Bill's typographic work into a simple two-style package. Wide Display and Wide Text variants provide differentiation across print and screen contexts. Supports 26 languages.
foundry_blurb: Sequel Rounded is a 20th-century neo-grotesque inspired by typefaces used by Max Bill, developed in collaboration with the Max Bill Georges Vantongerloo Foundation, emphasising direct and purposeful form and function for modern typography.

name: Spectators Headline
classification_notes: All-italic semi-informal sans with script-like qualities. Eight weights from Thin to Bold. Inspired by 1930s European poster lettering.
summary: A semi-informal all-italic sans with script-like character, drawing from 1930s European poster lettering across eight weights.
description: Spectators Headline presents an entirely italic design with friendly, script-influenced qualities rooted in European lettering from the 1930s. Eight weights from Thin through Bold provide strong expressive range, with a variable font included in the superfamily. Well suited to branding, packaging, advertising and editorial projects requiring warmth without losing polish. Supports 26 languages.
foundry_blurb: Spectators Headline is a semi-informal sans serif in an italic, all-lowercase design with script-like character, drawing inspiration from 1930s European lettering styles used in posters and packaging.
